reason: All hard gates PASS (Part B ok, loader=True, entrypoint=True). wm_inference=+0.0219 is positive and above v07d7 baseline (+0.0059) but below current research baseline +0.0269 (v07d13) by 0.005 — within run variance for same algorithm. Primary goal of v07d15 was memory fix + speedup validation, NOT wm_inference improvement. Memory fix validated: peak RAM 43.7GB vs v07d13 46GB (2.3GB savings from buffer removal). Speed: 92.4min vs v07d13 89.7min — gc.collect() per iter (~100 calls) adds overhead that offsets IL batch epoch-level speedup. Research baseline unchanged at +0.0269.

winner_margin_summary.verdict: run variance (same algo as v07d13; 0.005 gap is within noise)

memory_fix_validation.oom_prevented: yes — v07d14 OOM'd at 62.4GB; v07d15 at 100 iters peaks at 43.7GB
memory_fix_validation.main_ram_growth_source: NOT episode buffer (only 2.3GB); likely CUDA allocator caching + Python fragmentation
memory_fix_validation.note: Memory fix is necessary for 150-iter runs but margins are tight (~2GB headroom)

speedup_analysis.recommendation: remove gc.collect() from per-iter loop in v07d16; del _ro alone is sufficient

next_candidates: [aggressive] v07d16: extend N_ITERS=150 with memory fix (remove gc.collect per iter; del _ro alone is sufficient). 43.7GB at 100 iters → ~58-60GB at 150 iters (within 62GB).
[conservative] v07d16: N_ITERS=100 but remove gc.collect() per iter to recover the 2.7min overhead and confirm speedup.
[analysis] Investigate main RAM growth source (CUDA allocator / Python fragmentation) — may need torch.cuda.empty_cache() instead of gc.collect().
